records.title: Natural-deduction proof episode
records.admission_rationale: The instance derives a theorem from stated premises through explicit inference rules and a checkable proof trace.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: One finite natural-deduction derivation; metatheoretic completeness and human proof search are excluded.

candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: Formulas denote ordinary classical propositions; a proof state is valid only when each step is licensed by the registered calculus.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: The final theorem depends on the order and nesting of assumption and discharge events.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Derive Q→P from premise P and close the proof with a replayable derivation.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: A closed theorem is distinguished from open assumptions and rejected proof branches.
candidate_neutral_observations.uncertainty_and_revision: No probabilistic uncertainty; revision is limited to backtracking before proof closure.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: The record establishes only the stated derivation, not the behavior of all deductive reasoners.

formal_system.initial_state: goal Q -> P with premise P
formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: A closed theorem is distinguished from open assumptions and rejected proof branches.

records.title: Bayesian diagnostic update
records.admission_rationale: The instance updates explicit competing fault hypotheses from observed evidence using a frozen probabilistic model.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: Single exact Bayesian update over two hypotheses; parameter learning and causal discovery are excluded.

transitions_and_constraints: decision threshold is fixed before evidence
candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: Hypotheses are mutually exclusive faults; probabilities quantify modeled uncertainty under the frozen likelihood table.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: The posterior and decision depend on the recorded prior and evidence sequence.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Determine whether sensor failure or valve failure is the better-supported diagnosis after alarm evidence.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Competing diagnoses remain alternatives until the frozen decision rule selects one.
candidate_neutral_observations.uncertainty_and_revision: Uncertainty is explicit and revised by conditionalization; model uncertainty is outside scope.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: The result is conditional on the frozen model and does not establish real-world diagnostic accuracy.

formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Competing diagnoses remain alternatives until the frozen decision rule selects one.

records.title: Defeasible bird classification
records.admission_rationale: The instance applies defaults, records an exception, retracts an earlier conclusion, and preserves the reason for revision.

source_or_observation_contract.observation_boundary: Only the enumerated states, transitions, rules, inputs, outputs, and recorded history are in scope.
source_or_observation_contract.replay_requirement: A conforming evaluator must be able to replay every enumerated transition from the frozen initial state.

records.formalization_boundary: One finite priority-ordered default theory; open-world semantics beyond listed predicates are excluded.

transitions_and_constraints: retraction must cite the defeated support
candidate_neutral_observations.declared_semantics: Defaults license provisional conclusions; explicit exception facts defeat less specific rules.
candidate_neutral_observations.history_and_path_dependence: The accepted conclusion changes only after the exception event; both support histories are retained.
candidate_neutral_observations.objectives_or_tasks: Classify whether Tweety flies after learning that Tweety is a penguin.
candidate_neutral_observations.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Provisional and defeated conclusions are distinguished from the final accepted conclusion.
candidate_neutral_observations.uncertainty_and_revision: Revision is explicit: flies is retracted and not_flies is adopted.
candidate_neutral_observations.provenance_and_correspondence_limits: The example demonstrates revision under the frozen priority rule, not universal adequacy of that rule.

formal_system.commitment_or_alternative_structure: Provisional and defeated conclusions are distinguished from the final accepted conclusion.
